user_query:
I run a personal blog where I share my own success journeys, and I want my post metadata to better match what people search for. Using the attached input/posts.csv as the sole source of topics, please perform a light SEO audit focused on live SERP and produce structured recommendations and validations.

What to do for each row in input/posts.csv (columns: slug, title, meta_description, target_keyword, secondary_keywords where secondary keywords are pipe-delimited):

1) SERP collection (Internet required):
- Perform a web search for the exact target_keyword using a general-purpose search engine.
- Collect the first page of unique organic results (aim for up to 10; exclude obvious ads and video-only listings). For each result, capture: query (the exact target_keyword string), position (1-based organic order), url, title, meta_description (empty string if unavailable), title_length, meta_description_length, fetched_at (ISO 8601 timestamp).
- Save one JSON object per line to output/serp/raw_{slug}.jsonl.

2) Competitor scoring, filtering, and ranking:
- For each raw result, compute score as:
  +3 if target_keyword (case-insensitive) appears in title; +1 if it appears in meta_description; +1 if title_length is between 35 and 60 inclusive; +1 if meta_description_length is between 70 and 160 inclusive; +1 if the result’s domain contains any of: "blog", "medium", "substack", or the domain ends with ".blog".
- Sort by score (desc), then by original position (asc). Take the top 5.
- Write output/serp/top_competitors_{slug}.csv with columns: query, rank_by_score (1..N), original_position, score, url, title, meta_description, title_length, meta_description_length.

3) Recommendations (page optimization):
- For each slug, produce a recommended meta title and meta description that:
  - Include target_keyword as a case-insensitive substring in both fields.
  - Include at least one secondary keyword (from the pipe-delimited list) in the meta description.
  - Have lengths within: title 35–60 characters inclusive; meta description 110–160 characters inclusive.
- Create output/recommendations/{slug}_meta.json with fields: slug, target_keyword, secondary_keywords (array), recommended_title, recommended_meta_description, lengths { title, description }, sources (array of up to 2 urls taken from the corresponding top_competitors_{slug}.csv used as inspiration), timestamp (ISO 8601).
- Also aggregate all recommendations into output/recommendations/index.csv with columns: slug, target_keyword, recommended_title, recommended_title_length, recommended_meta_description, recommended_meta_description_length, sources (semicolon-separated URLs).

4) Validation (must run a local test/validation command):
- Implement and run a local script/command of your choice to validate that for every slug: (a) recommended_title contains target_keyword and length is 35–60; (b) recommended_meta_description contains target_keyword, contains at least one secondary keyword, and length is 110–160. Also validate that recommended titles are unique across all slugs.
- Produce output/validation_summary.json containing: an array of per-slug objects { slug, checks: { title_contains_target, title_length_ok, description_contains_target, description_contains_any_secondary, description_length_ok }, lengths { title, description }, violations: [failed_check_names] }, and a top-level all_titles_unique boolean.
- Write output/run_log.txt with the exact command you executed to generate validation_summary.json and the exit code on completion.

General notes and constraints:
- Use only the target_keyword string for queries; do not hardcode or assume specific sites. If a field is unavailable during SERP parsing, set it to an empty string and compute lengths accordingly (0 for empty).
- Keep file encodings UTF-8. Do not include screenshots; rely on structured data files.
- All deliverables must be created under the output/ directory as specified.

Deliverables to create:
- output/serp/raw_{slug}.jsonl (1 per slug)
- output/serp/top_competitors_{slug}.csv (1 per slug)
- output/recommendations/{slug}_meta.json (1 per slug)
- output/recommendations/index.csv (single aggregate)
- output/validation_summary.json (single)
- output/run_log.txt (single)

Input provided: input/posts.csv All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
